utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format contains five items: (1) health_coverage, (2) property_coverage, (3) travel_coverage, (4) additional_services, and (5) total_budget. In the final coverage plan, all five items are present. Now, check if each item is fully specified with all required details: health_coverage includes physical and mental health insurance with explicit costs—yes. property_coverage includes home/contents insurance with costs—yes. travel_coverage includes travel insurance with costs—yes. additional_services lists enhancement services but lacks explicit costs; the format required 'with costs,' so this one is incomplete. total_budget includes monthly and annual cost breakdown—yes. Therefore, 4 out of 5 items are fully specified according to the instructions.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists three main coverages under the same provider 'LifeFirst / InsurePlus'. To map them to the ground-truth ratings, we identify the closest matching rated items. For health_coverage, the provider pair 'LifeFirst / InsurePlus' corresponds to both physical and mental health components. The physical health rating for 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' is 9, and for 'InsurePlus Standard Plan' it is 10. Since the plan combines both under one provider, the AI assistant likely merged the top-rated available options, so we consider both. For property_coverage, 'LifeFirst / InsurePlus Comprehensive (replacement value)' matches the property protection options 'InsurePlus Standard Coverage' (rating 10) and 'LifeFirst Standard Coverage' (rating 9). For travel_coverage, 'LifeFirst / InsurePlus International (adventure activities included)' aligns with the mobility coverage 'InsurePlus Standard Plan' (rating 10) and 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' (rating 9). Therefore, the ratings mapped to each selected coverage reflect these corresponding rated options.
